pr.title: Melee decomp: GM menu and result matches
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Exact-match decompilation slice for Game-mode menu/result translation units, touching seven src/melee/gm files. The PR body claims 11 newly exact functions, 36 still-unmatched improvements, clean isolated/full regression reports, and git diff check success. Review discussion centered on avoiding fakematch pointer math, preferring natural loops, limiting pragmas, and requiring data-ownership evidence for float/string symbol use.

key_files.role: Major game-mode match work; diff shows cleanup of text rendering names, stat accumulation shapes, KO total names, repeated lookup helper extraction, natural loop conversion in fn_80167638, and PAD_STACK use.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gm_80160C90 var_f31/var_r5/var_r29 renamed to name_scale/use_alt_name/str
diff_excerpt: fn_80167638 changed from repeated FighterMatchInfo checks to for loop over ARRAY_SIZE
review r3410055128: pointer math called fakematch and probably a for loop

key_files.role: Game-mode match work with pragma review concern.
evidence_refs: review r3410056045: reviewer requested removing #pragma pool_data off

key_files.role: Game-mode match work involving animation setup helpers, float label reuse, and a narrowly scoped dont_inline pragma under review.

review r3409791039: follow-up says lbl_804DA808 is existing file-local 0.0f label used in nearby fn_8019035C setup paths

review r3409791078: follow-up says lbl_804DA810 is existing TU-owned float label and dont_inline was scoped to small wrapper/reset immediately

text_corpus bot report: gm_801BD028 reached 100.00%; several gm_1BA8 unmatched items improved

what_changed: Touched seven Game-mode menu/result translation units under src/melee/gm.
PR body reports 11 newly exact functions, 36 improvements in still-unmatched functions, and clean isolated/full regression reports.
gm_1601.c replaced temp/decompiler-style names in gm_80160C90 with role names such as name_scale, use_alt_name, and str.
gm_1601.c converted at least one repeated FighterMatchInfo search in fn_80167638 into a natural for loop over ARRAY_SIZE after review flagged pointer math/fakematch risk.
gm_1601.c introduced or retained matching tactics in several functions, including PAD_STACK and explicit ternary saturation forms instead of MIN/MAX macro forms in excerpts.
gm_18A5.c used existing float labels lbl_804DA808 and lbl_804DA810 in animation-call arguments, with follow-up comments documenting claimed TU-local data ownership.
gm_18A5.c added a scoped dont_inline pragma around fn_8019B860 according to review excerpt and follow-up rationale.
gm_1A4C.c added a static fixed-width char table lbl_803DB49C[][0xC], with follow-up evidence citing contiguous symbols and split ownership.
gm_1BA8.c moved some r3b byte accesses toward PlayerInitData casts/bitfields, but review still asked for more anti-pointer-math/type recovery.
gmresultplayer.c changed result-player animation/rank handling shapes; review suggested a probable for-loop reconstruction.

smart_moves.text: The PR body included narrow verification details: baseline hash, exact ninja command, slice regression report, full ship-set regression report, git diff check, and review_lint count.
Reviewer concerns about float/string symbol use were followed by explicit data-ownership rationale rather than treating generated labels as automatically acceptable.
The fn_80167638 review appears to have moved the code away from raw pointer math and back toward a typed ARRAY_SIZE loop over FighterMatchInfo.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_80167638 final excerpt uses for (i = 0; i < ARRAY_SIZE(info->FighterMatchInfo); i++)

smart_moves.text: Local variable names were improved where roles were evident, e.g. name_scale, use_alt_name, ko_stats, capped_total, ko_total, es_base, and match_end/is_teams/is_small_loser terms in excerpts/search terms.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When repeated field checks differ only by array index, prefer a natural loop over raw byte-pointer stepping or nested generated-looking branches.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_80167638 uses for loop and ARRAY_SIZE

decomp_lessons.lesson: Pointer math that walks known game structs should be treated as suspicious fakematch material until typed fields, arrays, or structs have been tried.
Replacing ordinary literals with labels is controversial unless there is concrete TU-local data ownership or repeated local use evidence; document that evidence in review.

Pragmas remain review-sensitive even when they help matching; comments show reviewers challenged both dont_inline and pool_data changes, and accepted rationale was tied to narrow scope plus clean regression evidence.
Expanded jobj.h-style assert lines should be checked against actual owning inline/helper behavior before converting them to local HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG calls.

naming_conventions.convention: Use short semantic locals when the role is evident from the surrounding code: name_scale, use_alt_name, str, ko_stats, capped_total, ko_total, es_base.
Keep address-style labels for data where semantic meaning is not established, but attach ownership/layout evidence before using them as source-level data objects.
evidence_refs: review r3409791039: lbl_804DA808 existing file-local float label
review r3409791078: lbl_804DA810 existing TU float label
review r3409791127: lbl_803DB49C fixed-width table layout

naming_conventions.convention: Helper names like fn_80164B48_check may be introduced for repeated checks, but the PR slice only proves local matching use, not a globally accepted semantic name.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: static inline bool fn_80164B48_check(u8 idx, u16* ptr)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used in multiple gm_1601.c functions to steer stack/codegen shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded evidence reports clean regressions, but individual PAD_STACK objdiff justification is not shown in the slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A narrow dont_inline pragma was added around fn_8019B860; follow-up says it was scoped to a small wrapper and reset immediately.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Do not generalize pragmas as normal style; reviewer also requested removing a pool_data pragma in gm_16F1.c.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Existing TU-owned float labels were used for 0.0f and 4.5f animation parameters in gm_18A5.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is evidence-specific to lbl_804DA808/lbl_804DA810 claims in this PR; standards still discourage creating fake data anchors.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A fixed-width char[][0xC] table was used to model a gm_1A4C.c-owned .data string block.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Treat as a source-update/path-fact candidate, not a blanket rule for string literals.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Some MIN/MAX macro forms were replaced by explicit ternary saturation expressions, apparently for codegen matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The slice does not include reviewer discussion proving these explicit forms should be preferred globally.

notes: Loaded PR body and metadata provide strong identity, scope, changed-file, merge, and verification claims.
Raw diff and review comments are truncated, so later revisions/resolutions for all 22 review comments are not fully visible.
PR body claims 11 newly exact functions, while the visible bot report excerpt says 10 new matches; this record preserves the PR-body claim and notes the discrepancy instead of resolving it.
Clean regression results are taken from the PR body and follow-up comments; no independent build or objdiff was run during intake.
The follow-up data-ownership statements for lbl_804DA808, lbl_804DA810, and lbl_803DB49C are treated as PR evidence, not independently verified path facts.
Reviewer intent beyond the quoted comments is not inferred.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer typed array loops over raw pointer stepping for repeated struct-slot searches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2686, review flagged fn_80167638 pointer math as a fakematch and probably a for loop; the visible diff shows the search over info->FighterMatchInfo expressed as a for loop using ARRAY_SIZE. This supports a reusable lesson to try natural typed loops before accepting byte-pointer stepping across known arrays.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Data-symbol literal replacements need explicit local ownership evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2686 review challenged replacing inline literals with lbl_804DA808/lbl_804DA810 and adding a fixed-width string table. Follow-up comments justified them with existing file-local/TU-local labels, nearby same-slot use, split ranges, symbols.txt stride, and clean regression reports. Use this as a candidate lesson: literal-to-symbol changes remain suspicious unless ownership/layout evidence is documented.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Pragma matching tactics need narrow scope and review-visible evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2686 contains both a challenged dont_inline pragma in gm_18A5.c and a reviewer request to remove pool_data off in gm_16F1.c. The accepted rationale shown for dont_inline depends on small-wrapper scope, immediate reset, existing local pattern, and clean regressions. This supports a candidate lesson to keep pragmas exceptional and evidence-backed.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1A4C.c fixed-width string table ownership candidate
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2686 follow-up states that gm_1A4C.c owns .data from 0x803DB430 to 0x803DBF78 and that symbols.txt already has contiguous lbl_803DB49C through lbl_803DB61C strings at 0xC stride, with the next symbol at 0x803DB628. Candidate path fact: lbl_803DB49C can be modeled as a gm_1A4C.c-owned fixed-width char[][0xC] table if independently verified against current splits/symbols.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-specific data ownership evidence, but intake cannot promote review-comment claims to source truth without curator verification.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_18A5.c existing float label reuse candidate
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2686 follow-up states lbl_804DA808 is an existing file-local 0.0f label used for the same fn_8019035C animation-parameter slot in nearby setup paths fn_80198D18 and fn_80198EBC, and lbl_804DA810 is an existing TU-owned 4.5f label. Candidate path fact if current symbols/source confirm this.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could guide future literal/data ownership review in this TU, but should be checked against current symbols and source.

source_update_candidates.title: Review example for anti-pointer-math plus natural-loop cleanup
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding PR #2686 as an example under existing natural-loops and typed-fields-over-pointer-math standards: reviewer identified byte pointer stepping over FighterMatchInfo as fakematch/probable for-loop, and the visible diff uses a typed ARRAY_SIZE loop.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is not a new standard; it is a possible concrete example for existing standards.

rejection_notes: Do not record a blanket rule that replacing float literals with lbl_* symbols is acceptable; this PR's rationale is tied to claimed existing TU-owned labels and same-slot usage.
Do not record a blanket rule that dont_inline pragmas are acceptable in gm files; evidence only supports a narrow, disputed exception in gm_18A5.c, while another pragma was explicitly challenged.
Do not infer that all review concerns were resolved; loaded review_comments and raw_diff are truncated.
Do not promote the PR-body verification as independently rerun validation.
Do not infer semantic names for address-style functions/symbols beyond names visible in the diff and comments.
